question: In Jesus forgiving sins before healing, what is the logic trap he sets for the scribes (visible vs invisible authority), and why is their charge (‘blasphemy’) actually the point?

choices.A: He links an invisible act (forgiving sins) to a visible verification (healing), forcing them to face his claimed authority; the ‘blasphemy’ charge highlights the divine-level claim.
choices.B: He avoids the issue of forgiveness by switching topics to healing.
choices.C: He admits the scribes are correct and retracts the forgiveness claim.
choices.D: He says prophets can forgive sins in the same way God does.

teaching.title: Visible proof of invisible authority
teaching.body: The scribes object because, in Jewish theology, God forgives sins. Jesus’ response sets a test: if he can do the visible miracle, it supports his claim to authority in the invisible realm. The narrative is designed to make the ‘blasphemy’ accusation the spotlight, not a footnote—because it clarifies what kind of authority Jesus is implying he has.
